results.classification_rationale: At least 2 confirmed de novo unrelated cases with CPVT1 phenotype: Patient 31 from Ohno2015 (paper evidence, de novo by trio) and an additional proband confirmed de novo by trio analysis (VCGS, ClinVar SCV003921799). Combined with gnomAD absence, RYR2 hotspot location, consistent in silico predictions, and ClinVar consensus of 3 diagnostic laboratory submissions (1P, 2LP), this meets the Pathogenic threshold for ≥2 de novo unrelated cases.
results.description: This variant has strong previous evidence of pathogenicity in unrelated individuals. RYR2 p.Y4725C has been identified in ≥2 unrelated individuals with catecholaminergic polymorphic ventricular tachycardia type 1 (CPVT1; MIM#604772), including confirmed de novo occurrences: one male with childhood onset (age 10) presenting with syncope (Ohno et al. 2015, PMID 26114861), and at least one additional de novo proband confirmed by trio analysis (VCGS, ClinVar SCV003921799). ClinVar shows aggregate Pathogenic/Likely Pathogenic classification (3 submissions: 1 Pathogenic, 2 Likely Pathogenic). The variant is absent from gnomAD and located in the C-terminal hotspot domain of RYR2.
results.notes:
**Summary:** Pathogenic. ClinVar aggregate P/LP (2 stars, 3 submissions: 1P, 2LP from diagnostic laboratories; no ClinGen expert panel). Key evidence is ≥2 confirmed de novo unrelated cases with CPVT1 (PS2 strong), meeting the Pathogenic threshold under the previous reports criteria. Additional supporting criteria: absent from gnomAD (PM2), C-terminal hotspot domain location (PM1), multiple in silico tools predicting damaging effect (PP3), and alternative amino acid substitutions at residue Y4725 (asparagine, histidine) also classified LP — consistent with PM5. No functional assay data identified (VCGS criterion 1007 explicitly notes no published functional evidence). No refuting evidence identified.

**De-duplication note:** Ohno2021 is a correction to Ohno2015 reporting the identical Patient 31; these constitute one de novo case, not two independent cases. The VCGS proband (ClinVar SCV003921799) is confirmed as a separate, independent de novo case by trio analysis. Total confirmed de novo unrelated cases: ≥2.

**Supporting evidence:**
- **Ohno2015**: [Patient 31 (male, age 28 at genetic analysis, onset age 10, syncope as most severe symptom)](#cite:Ohno2015 "31 | M | 28 | 10 | Syncope | 14174a>g | Y4725C | CT | Trio | de novo | none | none") carries p.Y4725C in the C-terminal domain, confirmed de novo by trio testing with both parents phenotypically unaffected. This is the primary paper-extracted case.
- **Ohno2021**: Correction to Ohno2015 [confirming identical Patient 31 data](#cite:Ohno2021 "31 | M | 28 | 10 | syncope | 14174a>g | Y4725C | CT | Trio | de novo | none | none") — same single case, not independent replication.
- **ClinVar**: Three diagnostic laboratory submissions. VCGS (SCV003921799, Pathogenic, evaluated 2024-10-11) applied detailed criteria: absent from gnomAD v2/v3/v4 (PM2), multiple in silico tools consistently predicting damaging with major amino acid change Tyr→Cys (PP3), alternative changes at Y4725 (to Asn and His) also classified LP in ClinVar/LOVD (PM5), prior reports of ≥2 unrelated CPVT1 individuals including one de novo in literature (PMIDs 23595086, 26114861, 29434162, 29453246; PM2_Moderate equivalent), and independently confirmed de novo in their own proband by trio analysis (PS2). Penetrance for CPVT1 estimated 60–70% (PMID 23549275). GeneDx (SCV005201507, LP, evaluated 2025-11-24) notes hotspot region location and cites additional supporting publications (PMIDs 29434162, 32152366, 34697415, 35135837, 37291213 — not included in paper extractions for this run). Labcorp (SCV001577112, LP, 2021) provides no detailed criteria. VCGS also explicitly notes dominant negative and gain-of-function as established mechanisms for CPVT1 in RYR2 (PMIDs 12459180, 27646203, 29477366).

papers.rank_rationale: Original publication reporting the confirmed de novo RYR2 p.Y4725C case (Patient 31) in CPVT1, the primary paper-based evidence underpinning the Pathogenic classification.
Correction to Ohno2015 confirming Patient 31 data; ranked second as it does not contribute independent case evidence.

claims.text: Patient 31 (male, age 28 at genetic analysis, onset age 10, syncope as most severe symptom) carries RYR2 c.14174a>g (p.Y4725C) in the C-terminal domain, confirmed de novo by trio testing with both parents phenotypically unaffected.
